definition: Knowing arising dependent on the mental faculty and dhammas.
notes: The project prefers mind-knowing because it preserves the analytical relation between mano and viññāṇa while aligning with the updated treatment of viññāṇa as knowing. Mind-consciousness remains an acceptable alternate where continuity with familiar doctrinal English matters.

context_rules.context: In most analytical and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose where the compound reads more naturally with a fuller adjective
In comparative doctrinal prose using established Buddhist-English terminology
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when continuity with standard terminology helps.

example_phrases.pali: mano ca paṭicca dhamme ca uppajjati manoviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on the mind and dhammas, mind-knowing arises
